request.question: Which Hesitation Wounds band member toured with Bruce Springsteen's band in 2009?

title: Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes
Rosalita (Come Out Tonight)
Jay Weinberg
Ed Manion
Frank Stefanko
The Fever (Bruce Springsteen song)
Hesitation Wounds
Wachovia Spectrum, Philadelphia, PA 10/20/09
Hesitation Marks
Hesitation Wounds (album)
sentences: Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes are a Jersey Shore musical group led by Southside Johnny.
They have been recording albums since 1976 and are closely associated with Bruce Springsteen & the E Street Band.
They have recorded and/or performed several Springsteen songs, including "The Fever" and "Fade Away".
Springsteen has also performed with the band on numerous occasions and in 1991 guested on their "Better Days" album.

During the band's formative years Miami Steve Van Zandt acted as the band's co-leader, guitarist, songwriter, arranger and producer while other E Streeters including Clarence Clemons, Max Weinberg, Garry Tallent, Ernest Carter, Patti Scialfa and Soozie Tyrell have all performed, toured and/or recorded with the Jukes.
The band's horn section – the Miami Horns – has also toured and recorded with Springsteen.
More than one hundred musicians can claim to have been members of the Asbury Jukes, including Jon Bon Jovi who toured with the band as a special guest during 1990.
Bon Jovi has also cited the band as an influence and Jukes' Bobby Bandiera and Jeff Kazee have also toured with Bon Jovi.

Other notable band members include Mark Pender and Richie "La Bamba" Rosenberg who have played regularly with the Max Weinberg 7 on both "Late Night with Conan O'Brien" and "The Tonight Show with Conan O'Brien".

"Rosalita (Come Out Tonight)" is a 1973 song by Bruce Springsteen, from his "The Wild, the Innocent & the E Street Shuffle" album, and is especially famed as a concert number for Springsteen and The E Street Band.
The song, which clocks in at just over seven minutes, is a story of forbidden love between the singer and the titular Rosalita, whose parents disapprove of his life in a rock and roll band.
It is included on the compilation albums "The Essential Bruce Springsteen" and "Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band Greatest Hits".
Jay Weinberg (born September 8, 1990), is an American musician and drummer.
He is the son of American drummer Max Weinberg.
He has played with the American punk band the Reveling and toured in 2009 as a drummer with Bruce Springsteen's E Street Band, substituting for his father.
During 2010, he was briefly the drummer for Madball.
During 2011 and 2012, Weinberg played with Against Me!
In 2014, Weinberg became the drummer for the American heavy metal music band Slipknot.

Ed Manion (born February 28, 1952), also known as Eddie "Kingfish" Manion, is an American saxophonist, who plays both tenor and baritone sax.As a solo artist, he released his own instrumental album titled Nightlife in 2015.
Manion is currently a recording and touring member of Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.

He was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band and also a member of Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour, later called Bruce Springsteen with The Sessions Band.
He is an original member of Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes, The Miami Horns, and Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.

As a session musician, he has recorded, toured, and/or performed with, among others, Bruce Springsteen and The E Street Band, Diana Ross, Gary U.S. Bonds, Bon Jovi, Willy DeVille, Dave Edmunds, Bob Dylan, Keith Richards, Darlene Love, Ronnie Spector, Dion, The Allman Brothers Band, Kim Wilson, and Graham Parker.
As a solo artist, he released his own CD titled "Follow Through" in 2004.
Frank Stefanko is a fine art photographer with connections to New Jersey performers Patti Smith and Bruce Springsteen.
Stefanko's early photographs, taken in the 1960s through the 1980s, reveal the emerging careers of the two young artists.
Frank retains an on-going working relationship with both Springsteen and Smith.
Stefanko will be releasing a book in November of 2017, entitled "Bruce Springsteen: Further Up the Road" that chronicles the 40-year working relationship between himself and Bruce Springsteen.

"The Fever" is an early Bruce Springsteen song, recorded and released on an extremely rare 7" demo to promote the band, coupled with 'Rendezvous', a live track recorded in New York, although the venue and date remain a mystery - Rendezvous opens with Bruce calling out "New York!
Go ahead, Max!"
The single was released under The Jersey Devil and was pressed on Bruce Records, which was Springsteen's own independent label for his band.
The Fever itself was performed by Springsteen in concert beginning in March 1973.

It was recorded in 914 Sound Studios in May 1973, during sessions for Springsteen's second album "The Wild, the Innocent & the E Street Shuffle", but was only used as a demo by manager Mike Appel's Laurel Canyon publishing arm and renamed "Fever For the Girl".
In early 1974 Appel sent it to several progressive rock radio stations, where it became a "turntable hit" in the ramp-up of Springsteen expectations prior to "Born to Run".

The original Springsteen recording finally achieved an official release surfacing in 1999 on his "18 Tracks" outtakes collection- the live b-side has never been officially released since in any other format, although a different live version is on 18 Tracks and Tracks (Bruce Springsteen album) and a studio cut of Rendezvous was released on 'The Promise' CD in 2010.
Hesitation Wounds is an American hardcore punk supergroup formed in 2012.

The band features vocalist Jeremy Bolm of Touché Amoré; guitarist Neeraj Kane of The Hope Conspiracy and formerly of The Suicide File and Holy Fever; bassist Stephen LaCour of True Cross and formerly of Trap Them; and Jay Weinberg, the son of Max Weinberg (E Street Band, The Max Weinberg 7), who previously performed with Madball and Against Me!
, and eventually joined Slipknot.
Wachovia Spectrum, Philadelphia, PA 10/20/09 is a live album by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band, released on July 13, 2017.
It is the fourteenth such release by the Bruce Springsteen Archives.

The performance was Springsteen's final show at the soon to be demolished Wachovia Spectrum in Philadelphia, PA and featured a full album performance of "Born in the U.S.A.", the show opener, "The Price You Pay", which was performed for the first time since 1981 and guest appearances by former band member Vini Lopez and even Springsteen's mother Adele.
Hesitation Marks is the eighth studio album by American industrial rock band Nine Inch Nails, released on August 30, 2013 by Columbia Records.
It is the band's first release through Columbia, and their first album released on a major record label since "Year Zero" (2007).
The album's title is derived from the term "hesitation wounds", which is used to describe marks that are produced by testing a bladed weapon before attempting suicide or self-harming.
Hesitation Wounds is the third studio album by American hip hop artist Sleep, a member of the Pacific Northwest hip hop collective Oldominion.
It was released on Strange Famous Records on June 29, 2009.

- Preserve the habit of extracting and citing concrete facts verbatim from the passages (names, dates, passage labels).
- Do not output a final answer, comparison, or any numeric fact that purports to resolve the question here. Even if the passages fully and unambiguously contain all required facts, present that evidence and explicitly state "no additional retrieval needed," but defer producing any final answer to a downstream component.

candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:

Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-focused second-hop query in the field `query`. Use only the provided `question` and `summary_1` as inputs. Do not include any explanation, answer, fallback text, lists, or additional fields — the output must be a single one-line query string.

When composing the query, select the minimal anchor token(s) visible in `question` and/or `summary_1` that point to the missing page or fact. You may append a single short attribute keyword (for example country member occupations record season) only when that keyword is needed to disambiguate the evidence type. Do not introduce any other new tokens.

Decision templates (choose the single template that matches the missing evidence implied by summary_1):
- Connector between two named items: emit a short interrogative naming both items and requesting the linking evidence (e.g., artist album recording) in one line. Retain the visible item tokens from the inputs.
- Metadata for a single anchor title: emit a compact keyword-style query consisting of the anchor plus brief attribute keywords (for example: artist genre).
- Temporal or aggregate fact: include the year or season token plus the entity type and a short cue such as season count.

Use these operational rules when choosing a template:
- If summary_1 explicitly lists two or more distinct page anchors as next-hop targets, combine those visible anchor tokens into one compact multi-anchor query and append a short attribute keyword naming the evidence type (for example country member occupations record).
- If summary_1 flags a single missing anchor or a missing_titles list is present, emit a single-anchor metadata-style query that places the missing anchor token first and appends a concise attribute keyword naming the desired fact.
- When summary_1 names multiple candidate entities and the missing fact concerns one candidate’s membership, order, or relation to a canonical collection, prefer a connector-style query that pairs the highest-priority candidate token (first or prioritized in summary_1) with the canonical collection token plus a short attribute keyword requesting membership or order.
- When individual member tokens are present but the needed retrieval is about that individual, prefer member-token + group-anchor + member (or equivalent short attribute) to target the individual’s page rather than issuing a broad group-level query.

Strict formatting rules:
- Emit exactly one line and exactly one string in `query` containing only the query text.
- Keep the query compact (aim for ≤ 10–12 words/tokens) and free of punctuation or explanatory clauses.
- Preserve visible tokens from `question` and `summary_1` (title words, years, or entity names) to anchor retrieval.
- Do not answer, conclude, enumerate candidates, assert hidden facts, or include user-facing instructions.

Produce the `query` field now.
candidate.summarize2.predict:

  - paraphrase up to one or two key attributes present in the passages (examples: nationality, award/winner status, current affiliation/team, partner identity, founding/formation fact, birth date), and
  - avoid long verbatim copying (prefer paraphrase; use short quotes only when a brief exact phrase from a passage is necessary for clarity).
- If the question requires naming both members of a partnership or collaborator pair, name both people in the summary and, if passages provide partner-specific attributes, include one brief attribute for each as separate short clauses or sentences if needed.
- Do not invent facts not present in the provided passages. If passages conflict on a specific attribute, do not synthesize a new reconciled fact; instead prefer the passage that most directly addresses the question or omit the disputed attribute (or state necessary ambiguity only when needed to avoid a false claim).
- Keep the `summary` compact and focused: avoid extraneous biography, discography/chart data, or unrelated commentary. When `context` is absent or is not a concise answer, produce a 1–2 sentence integrated summary naming the relevant entity(ies) and including at most one brief paraphrased attribute drawn from the passages.

Additional constraints (must follow):
- The first sentence must be an answer-first concise identification (a direct answer or concise identification). If the `context` already contains such a sentence, preserve it exactly unless a passage explicitly and directly contradicts it; otherwise derive that first sentence from the clearest passage.
- After the first sentence, append at most one short supporting sentence for each additional relevant named entity only when the passages supply corroborating attributes. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's canonical name as shown in the inputs (context or passages) and paraphrase no more than one or two simple attributes.
- When a queried binary/status attribute for an entity is not stated in the provided passages, indicate that absence concisely within that entity's supporting sentence (for example, with a short parenthetical or clause saying the status is not given in the provided evidence).
- If the passages explicitly assert a relationship between two or more entities that is directly relevant to the question, include one short relationship sentence after the per-entity supporting sentence(s) describing that relationship; do not embed the same relationship claim redundantly in multiple sentences.
- If relevant entities exceed the available supporting-sentence budget under the 1–3 sentence limit, prioritize entities in this order: entities explicitly named in the question, entities named in the preserved first sentence, then other context entities. Do not exceed the 1–3 sentence cap.

Output requirement: exactly one JSON field `summary` whose value is the integrated 1–3 sentence summary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Instructions:
- Use only `summary_1` and `summary_2` as the evidential basis for the final decision; do not add new facts or external information.
- First determine whether the question is a binary (yes/no) proposition and whether both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary outcome.
- If BOTH summaries unambiguously assert the same binary conclusion, emit exactly one lowercase token as the entire content of `answer`: use "yes" for an affirmative conclusion and "no" for a negative conclusion. The `answer` field must contain that single token and nothing else (no punctuation, no extra whitespace, no explanation).
- Prefer `summary_2` as the decisive statement when it clarifies or restates `summary_1`, but still require that both summaries support the same binary judgment before emitting the single-token `answer`.
- If the summaries conflict, if either summary hedges, is ambiguous, silent about the binary relation, or otherwise fails to clearly agree, do NOT apply the single-token rule; instead produce the module's normal fallback/unresolved output for `answer`.
- Do not infer that the final state is unresolved merely because `summary_1` uses earlier unresolved or bridge language; prefer `summary_2` when it clarifies `summary_1`.
- Always remain truthful to the visible summaries and do not alter their factual conclusions.

Binary detection is mandatory and must run first. Only if the question is not a binary yes/no or the binary detector does not apply, proceed to the extractive rules below. Use the extractor that matches the question intent and only apply an extractor when the summaries present the required adjacent or verbatim phrasing.

Extraction and formatting rules (apply only when binary detection does not produce a single-token answer):
- Person-name selection: if a longer verbatim personal-name string appears in either summary and contains a shorter candidate as a substring, emit the longest verbatim name exactly as it appears in the summaries. Prefer wording from `summary_2` when both summaries provide the same evidence but differ in form.
- Denomination/type extraction: for questions asking a "kind", "type", or "denomination" of a role, extract and emit exactly the minimal token or short phrase that appears immediately adjacent to the role word in the summaries (the word/phrase answering "what kind").
- Event-name extraction: when summaries show a contiguous proper-name followed by a generic event-type noun, emit only the contiguous proper-name core. Strip any leading articles and remove the trailing generic event-type noun; emit only the proper-name portion exactly as given in the summaries.
- Nationality handling: if both summaries unambiguously state the same nationality phrase and at least one summary places the literal single word "citizen" immediately after that nationality phrase, append exactly one space and the single word "citizen" to the nationality phrase. Otherwise emit the nationality phrase exactly as given in the summaries.

General formatting constraints for all outputs:
- Always emit the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries and do not add explanation, context, punctuation, or extra words.
- Do not introduce external facts, additional descriptors, dates, roles, or explanatory text not present in the summaries.
- Do not normalize or invent name variants that are not verbatim in the summaries.
- If the summaries conflict, lack the required adjacent phrasing, or do not present a clear verbatim form for the requested attribute, do not guess—use the module's normal unresolved output.

Output contract:
- Produce a single field `answer` whose contents follow the rules above. The `answer` field must contain only the minimal string supported by `summary_1` and `summary_2` and no additional tokens, punctuation, or explanation.

The band features vocalist Jeremy Bolm of Touché Amoré; guitarist Neeraj Kane of The Hope Conspiracy and formerly of The Suicide File and Holy Fever; bassist Stephen LaCour of True Cross and formerly of Trap Them; and Jay Weinberg, the son of Max Weinberg (E Street Band, The Max Weinberg 7), who previously performed with Madball and Against Me!

It is the band's first release through Columbia, and their first album released on a major record label since "Year Zero" (2007).
The album's title is derived from the term "hesitation wounds", which is used to describe marks that are produced by testing a bladed weapon before attempting suicide or self-harming.

response.hop1_query: Which Hesitation Wounds band member toured with Bruce Springsteen's band in 2009?
hop1_docs: Working on a Dream Tour | The Working on a Dream Tour was a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band, which began in April 2009 and ended in November 2009.  It followed the late January 2009 release of the album "Working on a Dream".  This was the first full E Street Band tour without founding member Danny Federici, who died during the previous tour in 2008, and the final tour for founding member Clarence Clemons, who died in 2011.

Ed Manion | Ed Manion (born February 28, 1952), also known as Eddie "Kingfish" Manion, is an American saxophonist, who plays both tenor and baritone sax.As a solo artist, he released his own instrumental album titled Nightlife in 2015.  Manion is currently a recording and touring member of Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.  He was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band and also a member of Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour, later called Bruce Springsteen with The Sessions Band.  He is an original member of Southside Johnny and the Asbury Jukes, The Miami Horns, and Little Steven and the Disciples of Soul.  As a session musician, he has recorded, toured, and/or performed with, among others, Bruce Springsteen and The E Street Band, Diana Ross, Gary U.S. Bonds, Bon Jovi, Willy DeVille, Dave Edmunds, Bob Dylan, Keith Richards, Darlene Love, Ronnie Spector, Dion, The Allman Brothers Band, Kim Wilson, and Graham Parker.  As a solo artist, he released his own CD titled "Follow Through" in 2004.

Bruce Springsteen 1992–1993 World Tour | The Bruce Springsteen 1992–1993 World Tour was a concert tour featuring Bruce Springsteen and a new backing band, that took place from mid-1992 to mid-1993.  It followed the simultaneous release of his albums "Human Touch" and "Lucky Town" earlier in 1992.  It was his first of four non-E Street Band tours.  Later, Springsteen had more non-E Street Band tours, the Ghost of Tom Joad Tour, the Seeger Sessions Tour, and the Devils & Dust Tour.  The tour was not as commercially or critically successful as past tours, due to poor reception of Human Touch and Lucky Town as well as changes from previous tours.  According to Springsteen biographer Dave Marsh, die-hard fans have informally referred to the backing band as "the Other Band" (and the tour as "The Other Band Tour").
Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour | Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour
Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour | Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour

Wrecking Ball World Tour | The Wrecking Ball World Tour was a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band to promote Springsteen's seventeenth studio album, "Wrecking Ball", which was released on March 5, 2012.  It was the first tour for the E Street Band without founding member Clarence Clemons, who died on June 18, 2011.  The world-wide tour in support of the album, which ended in September 2013, reached 26 countries, the most ever for one of Springsteen's tours.  The tour was resumed in January 2014 however this time it was to promote his new album, "High Hopes" and went under that album's name.
Magic Tour (Bruce Springsteen) | The Magic Tour was Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band's 2007–2008 concert tour of North America and Western Europe.
hop1_titles: Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour
Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band Reunion Tour
response.summary_1:
Primary topics: Bruce Springsteen 2009 tour; Ed Manion (saxophonist) cited as a Springsteen touring musician.

- "Working on a Dream Tour" — "began in April 2009 and ended in November 2009" and is "a concert tour by Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band."  
- Ed Manion (born "February 28, 1952") — "was a touring member of the horn section for Bruce Springsteen and the E Street Band" and a member of "Bruce Springsteen with The Seeger Sessions Band Tour."  
- Missing fact / bridge: The passages contain no mention of "Hesitation Wounds" or any Hesitation Wounds band member; the identity of the Hesitation Wounds member who toured with Springsteen in 2009 is not present.  
- Ambiguous candidate: Ed Manion appears as a 2009-era Springsteen touring musician in these passages but there is no evidence here that he is (or is not) a member of Hesitation Wounds.

Next-hop retrieval (priority order):
1. Fetch "Hesitation Wounds" (Wikipedia or band page) — inspect lede and "Members"/"Personnel" or "History" sections for member names and touring credits.  
2. For each Hesitation Wounds member named on that page, fetch their individual pages (or search "X Hesitation Wounds") — inspect lede/infobox and "Career"/"Touring" sections for links to Bruce Springsteen or 2009 tours.  
3. Fetch "Ed Manion" (Wikipedia) — inspect lede/infobox and "Career"/"Touring" to confirm any affiliation with Hesitation Wounds or specific 2009 tour roles.  
4. Fetch "Working on a Dream Tour" / "Working on a Dream Tour personnel" (section on band/horn personnel) to list touring horn members in 2009.
hop2_docs: Hesitation Wounds (album) | Hesitation Wounds is the third studio album by American hip hop artist Sleep, a member of the Pacific Northwest hip hop collective Oldominion.  It was released on Strange Famous Records on June 29, 2009.

Hesitation Wounds | Hesitation Wounds is an American hardcore punk supergroup formed in 2012.  The band features vocalist Jeremy Bolm of Touché Amoré; guitarist Neeraj Kane of The Hope Conspiracy and formerly of The Suicide File and Holy Fever; bassist Stephen LaCour of True Cross and formerly of Trap Them; and Jay Weinberg, the son of Max Weinberg (E Street Band, The Max Weinberg 7), who previously performed with Madball and Against Me! , and eventually joined Slipknot.

Hesitation Marks | Hesitation Marks is the eighth studio album by American industrial rock band Nine Inch Nails, released on August 30, 2013 by Columbia Records.  It is the band's first release through Columbia, and their first album released on a major record label since "Year Zero" (2007).  The album's title is derived from the term "hesitation wounds", which is used to describe marks that are produced by testing a bladed weapon before attempting suicide or self-harming.

The Hesitations | The Hesitations are an American R&B group from Cleveland, Ohio.  Formed in 1965, they scored several hits in 1967 and 1968, the biggest being their gospel-infused version of the title track to the movie "Born Free".  After one of the groups singers George "King" Scott was accidentally shot and killed in February 1968, the group disbanded.  The Hesitations regrouped in 2006 with new members and are performing and recording once again.
Neoregelia 'Hesitant' | 'Hesitant' is a hybrid cultivar of the genus "Neoregelia" in the Bromeliad family.
Maigret Hesitates | Maigret Hesitates (French: "Maigret hésite" ) is a detective novel by the Belgian writer Georges Simenon.
Hesitation step | The term hesitation step refers to a ceremonial form of walking.  It is typically used during the entrance to a religious ceremony.
